preview.summary: Four-time champions Germany open their Group E campaign against the smallest nation ever to reach a World Cup. Nagelsmann's side blend youth and experience and are heavy favourites, while debutants Curacao look to spoil the party through pace on the break.
preview.homeForm: Germany won five of six UEFA qualifiers to top their group and sit 10th in the FIFA rankings.
preview.awayForm: Curacao made history as the smallest nation by population ever to qualify, winning all four of their opening CONCACAF group games.
preview.homeKey: Florian Wirtz, creative spark linking with Jamal Musiala in attack.
preview.awayKey: Tahith Chong, Sheffield United winger and Curacao's main attacking threat.
storylines: Curacao are the smallest nation ever to play at a World Cup
78-year-old Dick Advocaat is the oldest coach in World Cup history
Manuel Neuer returns for a record-tying fifth World Cup
preview.h2h: The two nations have never met at senior international level.
